title: Use test-shift-aware stacking and post-hoc calibration instead of fixed raw-probability averaging
summary: Kaggle tabular writeups repeatedly warn that train/test shift breaks naive CV selection.
rationale: Kaggle tabular writeups repeatedly warn that train/test shift breaks naive CV selection. Your current blend is a simple XGB/CatBoost weighted average; a stronger variant is to detect shift, define more test-like folds/weights, then stack hazard/ranker/tree models with a small calibrator.
implementation_hint: Run adversarial validation on train vs. test using current engineered features; if AUC is meaningfully above 0.5, weight training rows by test-likeness or build validation from the most test-like races/drivers. Stack OOF predictions from pointwise, hazard, and ranking models with logistic/isotonic calibration on OOF only.
expected_effect: Better leaderboard transfer and smoother ensemble behavior than a fixed convex blend, especially if 2025 drivers/races differ from older seasons.
risk: Extra meta-modeling can overfit if the OOF protocol is not perfectly nested; isotonic can overfit if calibration folds are too small.
